name: Sun
constellation: Not in a constellation from Earth's perspective
description: Our local star is a medium-sized main sequence star whose energy supports nearly all life on Earth.

name: Sirius
description: Sirius is the brightest star in the night sky and shines with a crisp white-blue light.

name: Betelgeuse
description: Betelgeuse is a red supergiant nearing the end of its life, famous for its size and warm reddish glow.

name: Rigel
description: Rigel is a luminous blue supergiant whose intense heat makes it one of Orion's standout stars.

name: Polaris
description: Known as the North Star, Polaris sits close to the north celestial pole and helps navigators find direction.

name: Vega
description: Vega is a bright nearby star that once served as a key reference point for measuring stellar brightness.

name: Proxima Centauri
description: The closest known star to the Sun is a faint red dwarf with energetic flares and at least one intriguing exoplanet.

name: Aldebaran
description: Aldebaran appears as the eye of Taurus and is an orange giant star larger and cooler than the Sun.

name: Deneb
description: Deneb is a blue-white supergiant so luminous that it remains prominent despite its great distance.

name: Antares
description: Antares is a red supergiant with a fiery color that makes it one of the most recognizable stars of summer skies.

name: Orion Nebula
description: The Orion Nebula is a stellar nursery where new stars are forming inside glowing gas and dust.

name: Andromeda Galaxy
description: Our nearest large galactic neighbor contains hundreds of billions of stars and is visible to the naked eye in dark skies.
